criteria.criterion: `resolve_ticket_id` returns `None` for any id that is not a safe single path segment (rejects `../x`, absolute paths, `..`/`.`, `/`/`\` separators, and leading-dot names), and its fast path returns a separator-free canonical directory name — so no untrusted id escapes the tracker directory. Valid full/short/alias/Jira-key/prefix ids still resolve.

The audit sidecar readers in `completion_sidecar.py` and `plan_review/sidecar.py` no longer fall back to the raw unresolved id; when resolution fails they return their empty result instead of building a filesystem path from unvalidated input.
The workflow editor `/prompt` read (`_raw_prompt_text`) cannot read a file outside `<repo>/.rebar/prompts/`; a traversing prompt id yields no file (returns `None`/404).
The review-bot `/rerun` 502 response body contains no exception/stack-trace text; the detail is logged server-side.

New unit tests assert traversal rejection for the resolver, one sidecar reader, and the editor prompt read, and assert the review-bot 502 body carries no exception detail. `make lint` and the affected test suites pass.
